{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_359f01698c4e4dcaa6f55432","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n2\t\n3\t**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n4\t\n5\tMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n6\t\n7\t_Last updated: 2026-05-02 | Status: Active_\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Table of Contents\n12\t\n13\t- [Preamble](#preamble) — North Star, Background, Non-Goals, Normative Language, Scope Lock\n14\t- [Acceptance Scenarios](#acceptance-scenarios) — Definition of done, five verifiable scenarios\n15\t- [Implementation Phasing](#implementation-phasing) — Phase gates, walking skeleton, completion criteria\n16\t1. [Design Principles](#1-design-principles)\n17\t2. [Architecture](#2-architecture)\n18\t3. [Document, Index, and Search Lifecycle](#3-document-index-and-search-lifecycle)\n19\t4. [Implementation](#4-implementation)\n20\t5. [API Surface and Compatibility](#5-api-surface-and-compatibility)\n21\t6. [Deployment](#6-deployment)\n22\t7. [CI/CD](#7-cicd)\n23\t8. [Testing](#8-testing)\n24\t9. [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n25\t10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n26\t11. [Onboarding](#11-onboarding)\n27\t12. [Delivered Artifacts](#12-delivered-artifacts)\n28\t13. [Advanced Capabilities](#13-advanced-capabilities)\n29\t14. [Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling](#14-resource-envelope-and-horizontal-scaling)\n30\t15. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n31\t16. [Risk Register](#16-risk-register)\n32\t\n33\t---\n34\t\n35\t## Preamble\n36\t\n37\t### North Star\n38\t\n39\tMiroir succeeds when an application already using standard Meilisearch SDKs can scale its search index beyond a single node's capacity — without modifying a single line of application code — and achieve search latency no worse than 2× what a single Meilisearch CE node delivers for the same query on the same corpus.\n40\t\n41\t### Background\n42\t\n43\tMeilisearch Community Edition (CE) is a single-node search engine. It stores the entire index in RAM and on one disk. When a corpus grows beyond what one machine can hold, or when query throughput saturates a single node's CPU, operators have only two options: migrate to Meilisearch Enterprise (licensed, per-node pricing) or build a bespoke sharding layer from scratch. Neither is good for small teams and cost-sensitive deployments.\n44\t\n45\tMiroir provides the third option: a thin, stateless orchestration proxy that stripes documents across any number of CE nodes using the same rendezvous hashing algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally. It is invisible to application code — clients talk to one endpoint using the unchanged Meilisearch REST API.\n46\t\n47\tThe immediate motivation is the need to shard a large product catalog and log corpus across Meilisearch CE nodes on Rackspace Spot without purchasing an Enterprise license or rebuilding the application search layer.\n48\t\n49\t### Non-Goals (v0.x)\n50\t\n51\tThese are consciously excluded. Adding any of them requires a plan section update **before** implementation begins.\n52\t\n53\t| # | Non-Goal | Rationale |\n54\t|---|----------|-----------|\n55\t| NG-1 | Data-plane tenant isolation | §13.15 provides routing affinity (noisy-neighbor reduction), not data isolation. True tenant isolation — separate keyspace, separate auth, separate encryption — requires a fundamentally different trust model and is Meilisearch Enterprise territory. |\n56\t| NG-2 | Non-Meilisearch backends | Miroir uses Meilisearch CE's specific REST API, filter syntax, and `_rankingScore` semantics. Supporting Elasticsearch, Typesense, or OpenSearch would require per-backend adapters with no shared path. |\n57\t| NG-3 | Automatic primary key inference | Miroir requires an explicit primary key at index creation. Meilisearch's inference-from-document-body is unavailable because shard routing must be deterministic before examining document content. |\n58\t| NG-4 | arm64 support in v0.x | The deployment fleet is amd64-only. arm64 cross-compilation is deferred until a concrete deployment target emerges. See §15 OP#6. |\n59\t| NG-5 | Meilisearch Enterprise feature parity | Miroir targets CE. Features requiring Enterprise behavior (native multi-tenancy at the CE level, composite index) are not planned. |\n60\t| NG-6 | Automatic CE major-version migration | Miroir forwards schema/settings changes but does not manage migrations between incompatible Meilisearch CE major versions. Operators perform CE major upgrades independently. |\n61\t| NG-7 | Multi-region active-active replication | Replica groups scale throughput within one datacenter. Cross-region replication with conflict resolution is out of scope. |\n62\t\n63\t### What Miroir Is NOT\n64\t\n65\t- **NOT a Meilisearch fork or patch.** Every Meilisearch node runs unmodified Community Edition. Miroir only uses the public REST API.\n66\t- **NOT a service mesh or sidecar.** Miroir is a standalone proxy Deployment, not a per-node sidecar. It does not require Istio or Envoy.\n67\t- **NOT a database replication system.** There is no leader election among Meilisearch nodes, no Raft, no Paxos. Consistency is achieved through orchestrated fan-out and anti-entropy repair.\n68\t- **NOT a cache layer.** Query coalescing (§13.10) deduplicates identical concurrent requests but does not cache results across time. Results are always fetched live from nodes.\n69\t- **NOT safe to bypass.** Writing directly to Meilisearch nodes while Miroir is running produces inconsistent shard assignments that anti-entropy cannot repair (it has no knowledge of writes it did not route). All writes MUST go through Miroir.\n70\t- **NOT a replacement for Meilisearch Enterprise SLAs.** Miroir is open-source software with no guaranteed support contract.\n71\t\n72\t### Normative Language\n73\t\n74\tThe key words in this document follow RFC 2119:\n75\t\n76\t- **MUST / MUST NOT** — absolute requirement; implementation fails the spec if violated\n77\t- **SHOULD / SHOULD NOT** — recommended; deviation requires a documented rationale in the code or a linked ADR\n78\t- **MAY** — truly optional; no justification needed either way\n79\t\n80\tWhen these words appear capitalized, they are binding. Lowercase usage is descriptive prose.\n81\t\n82\t### Scope Lock Doctrine\n83\t\n84\t1. **During implementation:** Gaps or ambiguities in this plan may be resolved inline. New features or architectural changes require a plan update **and a bead update** before implementation — not after.\n85\t2. **Non-goal additions:** Add to the Non-Goals table above. Do not implement without removing from the table first (removing means accepting the scope).\n86\t3. **§13 Advanced Capabilities:** These are individually togglable and can be deferred without a plan change. Deferral is tracked in the bead for the relevant phase.\n87\t4. **Breaking changes:** Any change to the public Meilisearch API contract, the task store schema, or the Helm values schema is a breaking change and MUST result in a MAJOR version bump and CHANGELOG entry before merging.\n88\t\n89\t### Revision History\n90\t\n91\t| Date | Change |\n92\t|------|--------|\n93\t| 2026-05-02 | Added Preamble (North Star, Background, Non-Goals, Normative Language, Scope Lock). Added Acceptance Scenarios, Implementation Phasing, System Invariants (§4), Anti-Patterns Catalog (§4), property-based test spec (§8), Benchmark Denominator Contract (§8), Definition of Done (§8). Updated OP#4 with Phase 9 exit-criterion deadline. Added Risk Register (§16). |\n94\t| (initial) | Initial plan document |\n95\t\n96\t---\n97\t\n98\t## 1. Design Principles\n99\t\n100\t1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n101\t2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n102\t3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n103\t4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n104\t5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n105\t6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n106\t7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n107\t8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n108\t\n109\t### Key constraints\n110\t\n111\t- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n112\t- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n113\t- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n114\t- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n115\t\n116\t---\n117\t\n118\t## Acceptance Scenarios\n119\t\n120\tThese are the definition of \"done\" for Miroir. Each scenario is independently verifiable. Each maps to a phase completion gate (cross-referenced in [Implementation Phasing](#implementation-phasing)).\n121\t\n122\t### Scenario 1: Invisible Federation (Happy Path)\n123\t**Setup:** 3-node Meilisearch CE cluster. Miroir with S=16, RF=1, RG=1. Standard Python Meilisearch SDK pointed at Miroir's endpoint.\n124\t**Action:** Developer changes only the endpoint URL in their existing SDK client. Indexes 10,000 product documents with `product_id` as primary key. Executes 5 distinct search queries.\n125\t**Pass criteria:**\n126\t- HTTP 200 on all 5 queries; response JSON validates against the Meilisearch search response schema\n127\t- API compatibility test suite (§8) passes against both Miroir and a single-node Meilisearch with identical results for each query\n128\t- `GET /_miroir/topology` shows all 3 nodes healthy, `fully_covered: true`\n129\t- No `X-Miroir-Degraded` header on any response\n130\t**Fail criteria:**\n131\t- Any field is absent that Meilisearch would return (`estimatedTotalHits`, `facetDistribution`, etc.)\n132\t- Any standard Meilisearch SDK raises an exception that would not arise against a direct CE instance\n133\t- Search result set differs in top-3 ordering from what single-node Meilisearch returns for identical documents and settings\n134\t\n135\t### Scenario 2: Node Failure Tolerance (HA)\n136\t**Setup:** 3-node cluster, RF=2, RG=1, S=32. 50,000 documents indexed and confirmed searchable.\n137\t**Action:** Operator terminates one of the 3 Meilisearch pods while search and write traffic continues.\n138\t**Pass criteria:**\n139\t- 100% of searches during the failure window return HTTP 200 with complete results; `X-Miroir-Degraded` is absent (RF=2 means surviving replicas cover all shards)\n140\t- Writes during the failure window return HTTP 200 (at least one group reached quorum)\n141\t- `GET /_miroir/topology` transitions from `healthy` → `degraded` within 15s of termination, and back to `healthy` within 15s of recovery\n142\t- Anti-entropy closes any replica divergence introduced during the failure within one scheduled pass\n143\t**Fail criteria:**\n144\t- Any HTTP 503 during the single-node failure window with RF=2 (full shard coverage is maintained)\n145\t- `X-Miroir-Degraded` header appears after the failed node recovers and routing resumes\n146\t\n147\t### Scenario 3: Node Addition Without Downtime (Elastic Scale)\n148\t**Setup:** 2-node cluster, S=16, RF=1, RG=1. 100,000 documents indexed.\n149\t**Action:** Operator scales the Meilisearch StatefulSet to 3 replicas and registers the new node via `POST /_miroir/nodes`.\n150\t**Pass criteria:**\n151\t- Rebalance completes within `migration_timeout_s` for 100k documents\n152\t- `miroir_degraded_shards_total = 0` throughout the rebalance\n153\t- Document count via `GET /indexes/products/stats` is unchanged before and after rebalance\n154\t- No search returns zero results for any document that was indexed before the rebalance\n155\t- `miroir-ctl verify` reports 0 discrepancies after rebalance completes\n156\t**Fail criteria:**\n157\t- Any document present before rebalance is unreachable via search or `GET /documents/{id}` after rebalance\n158\t- `miroir_degraded_shards_total > 0` for more than 5 minutes during migration\n159\t- Any node holds > 60% of total documents after rebalance (non-uniform distribution)\n160\t\n161\t### Scenario 4: Settings Change Propagates Consistently\n162\t**Setup:** 3-node cluster, RF=2, S=32. 10,000 documents indexed.\n163\t**Action:** Operator adds a synonym: `[\"laptop\", \"notebook\"]`. Waits for the Miroir task to reach `succeeded`. Immediately searches for \"notebook\".\n164\t**Pass criteria:**\n165\t- `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` on each Meilisearch node directly returns identical `synonyms` objects after the task succeeds\n166\t- Search for \"notebook\" returns ≥ 1 hit within 5s of task completion\n167\t- `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` is absent on all responses after the task reaches `succeeded`\n168\t- `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total` does not increment after the two-phase broadcast commits\n169\t**Fail criteria:**\n170\t- Any Meilisearch node has different synonyms than others after the commit phase (checked via direct `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` on each node)\n171\t- Search for \"notebook\" returns zero hits while search for \"laptop\" returns > 0 hits on the same index\n172\t\n173\t### Scenario 5: Migration from Single-Node (Operator Journey)\n174\t**Setup:** Existing single-node Meilisearch CE instance with a ~5 GB corpus, ~500k documents, running in production.\n175\t**Action:** Operator follows the §11 \"dump and reload\" migration path: exports a dump, deploys Miroir + 3-node cluster, imports via `POST /_miroir/dumps/import`.\n176\t**Pass criteria:**\n177\t- `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` via Miroir returns `numberOfDocuments ≥ 499,000` after import task succeeds (< 0.2% loss threshold)\n178\t- 20 randomly-sampled queries return identical top-3 results against both the original instance and Miroir (with identical settings)\n179\t- `miroir-ctl verify` reports 0 discrepancies after import\n180\t- No single node holds > 60% of total documents (shard distribution confirmed)\n181\t**Fail criteria:**\n182\t- `numberOfDocuments` after import < 499,000\n183\t- Any of the 20 sampled queries differs in top-3 hits against Miroir vs. the original instance\n184\t- Any node holds > 60% of documents (broadcast mode accidentally used instead of streaming)\n185\t\n186\t---\n187\t\n188\t## Implementation Phasing\n189\t\n190\tImplementation is broken into 12 phases. The bead tracker (`.beads/`) is the authoritative task list; this section defines **phase gates only** — the testable criteria that determine when each phase is complete and the next may begin.\n191\t\n192\t### Phase 0: Foundation (Walking Skeleton) — COMPLETE\n193\t**Delivers:** Cargo workspace, all three crates scaffold. Binary compiles, boots, and serves `/health → {\"status\":\"available\"}`. Config struct loads from YAML.\n194\t**Completion criteria (all must pass on same commit):**\n195\t- `cargo build --all` succeeds with zero warnings\n196\t- `GET /health` returns `{\"status\":\"available\"}`\n197\t- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean\n198\t\n199\t### Phase 1: Core Routing — COMPLETE\n200\t**Delivers:** Rendezvous hash, shard assignment, covering-set derivation, result merger. Pure functions — no HTTP.\n201\t**Completion criteria:**\n202\t- `cargo test --package miroir-core` — 100% of router, topology, scatter, merger unit tests pass\n203\t- Determinism: same inputs always produce same shard assignment (unit test)\n204\t- Minimal reshuffling: adding a node moves at most ⌈S/(N+1)⌉ shards (unit test)\n205\t\n206\t### Phase 2: HTTP Proxy + API Surface — COMPLETE\n207\t**Delivers:** Full Meilisearch-compatible HTTP proxy — write fan-out, scatter-gather search, task reconciliation, auth dispatch.\n208\t**Completion criteria:**\n209\t- All route handler integration tests pass against a single-node Meilisearch (RF=1, S=16)\n210\t- Scenario 1 (Invisible Federation) passes manually\n211\t\n212\t### Phase 3: Task Store + Persistence — COMPLETE\n213\t**Delivers:** SQLite task store with all 14 tables. Task IDs survive Miroir restarts.\n214\t**Completion criteria:**\n215\t- SQLite: all tables created on startup; task mapping survives process restart (unit test simulating restart)\n216\t- `cargo test --package miroir-core -- task` passes\n217\t\n218\t### Phase 4: Topology Operations — IN PROGRESS\n219\t**Entry criteria:** Phase 3 complete; task store persists durably.\n220\t**Delivers:** Rebalancer, node add/remove, replica group add/remove, drain, health-check state machine.\n221\t**Completion criteria:**\n222\t- Scenario 3 (Node Addition Without Downtime) passes against a 3-node docker-compose setup\n223\t- Node drain completes with `miroir_degraded_shards_total = 0` after drain finishes\n224\t- `miroir-ctl verify` reports 0 discrepancies after each rebalance\n225\t\n226\t### Phase 5: Advanced Capabilities (§13.1–§13.21) — OPEN\n227\t**Entry criteria:** Phase 4 complete; Scenario 3 passes.\n228\t**Delivers:** §13.1 resharding, §13.5 two-phase broadcast, §13.6 session pinning, §13.7 aliases, §13.8 anti-entropy, §13.9 dump import. All individually feature-flagged; remaining §13.x capabilities follow.\n229\t**Completion criteria (minimum for phase exit):**\n230\t- §13.5: Scenario 4 (Settings Change) passes\n231\t- §13.9: Scenario 5 (Migration) passes for a 100k-document corpus\n232\t- Each enabled capability has ≥ 1 integration test scenario passing\n233\t- Toggling any capability to `enabled: false` restores pre-capability behavior (regression-tested)\n234\t\n235\t### Phase 6: Horizontal Scaling (§14) — OPEN\n236\t**Entry criteria:** Phase 5 complete; §13.5 and §13.9 pass their scenarios.\n237\t**Delivers:** Peer discovery, Mode A/B/C background work partitioning, HPA spec, Redis task store.\n238\t**Completion criteria:**\n239\t- 2-pod Miroir + Redis: anti-entropy, TTL sweeper, and canary runner each run on exactly one pod at a time (verified via `miroir_owned_shards_count` metric)\n240\t- HPA scales from 2 → 4 pods under synthetic load; task ownership redistributes without duplicate work\n241\t- `values.schema.json` rejects `replicas > 1` + `taskStore.backend=sqlite`\n242\t\n243\t### Phase 7: Observability — COMPLETE\n244\t**Delivers:** Core metric families, Grafana dashboard, ServiceMonitor, PrometheusRule.\n245\t\n246\t### Phase 8: Deployment + CI — COMPLETE\n247\t**Delivers:** Dockerfile, Helm chart, Argo Workflows CI template, ArgoCD Application manifest.\n248\t\n249\t### Phase 9: Testing — OPEN\n250\t**Entry criteria:** Phase 6 complete; all capabilities implemented.\n251\t**Delivers:** Full test suite — unit (≥90% core coverage), integration, API compatibility, chaos, property-based, CI-gated benchmarks.\n252\t**Completion criteria:**\n253\t- Unit coverage ≥ 90% for `miroir-core` (tarpaulin gate passes in CI)\n254\t- All 5 acceptance scenarios (§ \"Acceptance Scenarios\") pass\n255\t- All 6 chaos scenarios in §8 pass\n256\t- Property-based tests for router, merger, and filter parser pass (§8 property-based tests)\n257\t- CI benchmark step enforces p99 search latency < 2× single-node on the reference corpus (§8 benchmark denominator) — hard CI failure, not advisory\n258\t- **OP#4 exit criterion:** score normalization validated empirically at ≥ 5M and ≥ 50M documents on the reference corpus before Phase 9 may be declared complete (see §15 OP#4)\n259\t\n260\t### Phase 10: Security + Secrets — OPEN\n261\t**Entry criteria:** Phase 9 complete.\n262\t**Delivers:** Full secret rotation flows, CSRF, scoped Meilisearch key lifecycle.\n263\t**Completion criteria:**\n264\t- All 5 secret rotation procedures in §9 are manually exercised and recorded in CHANGELOG\n265\t- CSRF protection verified via automated cross-origin state-mutating request (rejected 403)\n266\t- `miroir-ctl` non-interactive mode (`--yes` / `MIROIR_NON_INTERACTIVE=1`) works for all destructive commands\n267\t\n268\t### Phase 11: Onboarding + Docs + Artifacts — OPEN\n269\t**Entry criteria:** Phase 10 complete.\n270\t**Delivers:** README, CHANGELOG, SDK examples, public Helm repo.\n271\t**Completion criteria:** Scenario 1 runs end-to-end using only the README quick-start guide, with no prior knowledge of this plan document.\n272\t\n273\t---\n274\t\n275\t## 2. Architecture\n276\t\n277\t### Core model\n278\t\n279\tMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n280\t\n281\t- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n282\t- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n283\t- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n284\t- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n285\t- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n286\t- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n287\t- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n288\t- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n289\t- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n290\t\n291\t```\n292\tClient\n293\t  │\n294\t  ▼\n295\tMiroir Orchestrator\n296\t  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n297\t  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n298\t  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n299\t\n300\t  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"af2eb62b-725a-4fcb-ab57-17c1e0686a49","uuid":"40024549-eb43-486a-b87d-bfa462d335b4","timestamp":"2026-05-02T20:37:17.772Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n\n**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n\nMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n\n_Last updated: 2026-05-02 | Status: Active_\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n- [Preamble](#preamble) — North Star, Background, Non-Goals, Normative Language, Scope Lock\n- [Acceptance Scenarios](#acceptance-scenarios) — Definition of done, five verifiable scenarios\n- [Implementation Phasing](#implementation-phasing) — Phase gates, walking skeleton, completion criteria\n1. [Design Principles](#1-design-principles)\n2. [Architecture](#2-architecture)\n3. [Document, Index, and Search Lifecycle](#3-document-index-and-search-lifecycle)\n4. [Implementation](#4-implementation)\n5. [API Surface and Compatibility](#5-api-surface-and-compatibility)\n6. [Deployment](#6-deployment)\n7. [CI/CD](#7-cicd)\n8. [Testing](#8-testing)\n9. [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n11. [Onboarding](#11-onboarding)\n12. [Delivered Artifacts](#12-delivered-artifacts)\n13. [Advanced Capabilities](#13-advanced-capabilities)\n14. [Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling](#14-resource-envelope-and-horizontal-scaling)\n15. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n16. [Risk Register](#16-risk-register)\n\n---\n\n## Preamble\n\n### North Star\n\nMiroir succeeds when an application already using standard Meilisearch SDKs can scale its search index beyond a single node's capacity — without modifying a single line of application code — and achieve search latency no worse than 2× what a single Meilisearch CE node delivers for the same query on the same corpus.\n\n### Background\n\nMeilisearch Community Edition (CE) is a single-node search engine. It stores the entire index in RAM and on one disk. When a corpus grows beyond what one machine can hold, or when query throughput saturates a single node's CPU, operators have only two options: migrate to Meilisearch Enterprise (licensed, per-node pricing) or build a bespoke sharding layer from scratch. Neither is good for small teams and cost-sensitive deployments.\n\nMiroir provides the third option: a thin, stateless orchestration proxy that stripes documents across any number of CE nodes using the same rendezvous hashing algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally. It is invisible to application code — clients talk to one endpoint using the unchanged Meilisearch REST API.\n\nThe immediate motivation is the need to shard a large product catalog and log corpus across Meilisearch CE nodes on Rackspace Spot without purchasing an Enterprise license or rebuilding the application search layer.\n\n### Non-Goals (v0.x)\n\nThese are consciously excluded. Adding any of them requires a plan section update **before** implementation begins.\n\n| # | Non-Goal | Rationale |\n|---|----------|-----------|\n| NG-1 | Data-plane tenant isolation | §13.15 provides routing affinity (noisy-neighbor reduction), not data isolation. True tenant isolation — separate keyspace, separate auth, separate encryption — requires a fundamentally different trust model and is Meilisearch Enterprise territory. |\n| NG-2 | Non-Meilisearch backends | Miroir uses Meilisearch CE's specific REST API, filter syntax, and `_rankingScore` semantics. Supporting Elasticsearch, Typesense, or OpenSearch would require per-backend adapters with no shared path. |\n| NG-3 | Automatic primary key inference | Miroir requires an explicit primary key at index creation. Meilisearch's inference-from-document-body is unavailable because shard routing must be deterministic before examining document content. |\n| NG-4 | arm64 support in v0.x | The deployment fleet is amd64-only. arm64 cross-compilation is deferred until a concrete deployment target emerges. See §15 OP#6. |\n| NG-5 | Meilisearch Enterprise feature parity | Miroir targets CE. Features requiring Enterprise behavior (native multi-tenancy at the CE level, composite index) are not planned. |\n| NG-6 | Automatic CE major-version migration | Miroir forwards schema/settings changes but does not manage migrations between incompatible Meilisearch CE major versions. Operators perform CE major upgrades independently. |\n| NG-7 | Multi-region active-active replication | Replica groups scale throughput within one datacenter. Cross-region replication with conflict resolution is out of scope. |\n\n### What Miroir Is NOT\n\n- **NOT a Meilisearch fork or patch.** Every Meilisearch node runs unmodified Community Edition. Miroir only uses the public REST API.\n- **NOT a service mesh or sidecar.** Miroir is a standalone proxy Deployment, not a per-node sidecar. It does not require Istio or Envoy.\n- **NOT a database replication system.** There is no leader election among Meilisearch nodes, no Raft, no Paxos. Consistency is achieved through orchestrated fan-out and anti-entropy repair.\n- **NOT a cache layer.** Query coalescing (§13.10) deduplicates identical concurrent requests but does not cache results across time. Results are always fetched live from nodes.\n- **NOT safe to bypass.** Writing directly to Meilisearch nodes while Miroir is running produces inconsistent shard assignments that anti-entropy cannot repair (it has no knowledge of writes it did not route). All writes MUST go through Miroir.\n- **NOT a replacement for Meilisearch Enterprise SLAs.** Miroir is open-source software with no guaranteed support contract.\n\n### Normative Language\n\nThe key words in this document follow RFC 2119:\n\n- **MUST / MUST NOT** — absolute requirement; implementation fails the spec if violated\n- **SHOULD / SHOULD NOT** — recommended; deviation requires a documented rationale in the code or a linked ADR\n- **MAY** — truly optional; no justification needed either way\n\nWhen these words appear capitalized, they are binding. Lowercase usage is descriptive prose.\n\n### Scope Lock Doctrine\n\n1. **During implementation:** Gaps or ambiguities in this plan may be resolved inline. New features or architectural changes require a plan update **and a bead update** before implementation — not after.\n2. **Non-goal additions:** Add to the Non-Goals table above. Do not implement without removing from the table first (removing means accepting the scope).\n3. **§13 Advanced Capabilities:** These are individually togglable and can be deferred without a plan change. Deferral is tracked in the bead for the relevant phase.\n4. **Breaking changes:** Any change to the public Meilisearch API contract, the task store schema, or the Helm values schema is a breaking change and MUST result in a MAJOR version bump and CHANGELOG entry before merging.\n\n### Revision History\n\n| Date | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| 2026-05-02 | Added Preamble (North Star, Background, Non-Goals, Normative Language, Scope Lock). Added Acceptance Scenarios, Implementation Phasing, System Invariants (§4), Anti-Patterns Catalog (§4), property-based test spec (§8), Benchmark Denominator Contract (§8), Definition of Done (§8). Updated OP#4 with Phase 9 exit-criterion deadline. Added Risk Register (§16). |\n| (initial) | Initial plan document |\n\n---\n\n## 1. Design Principles\n\n1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n\n### Key constraints\n\n- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Scenarios\n\nThese are the definition of \"done\" for Miroir. Each scenario is independently verifiable. Each maps to a phase completion gate (cross-referenced in [Implementation Phasing](#implementation-phasing)).\n\n### Scenario 1: Invisible Federation (Happy Path)\n**Setup:** 3-node Meilisearch CE cluster. Miroir with S=16, RF=1, RG=1. Standard Python Meilisearch SDK pointed at Miroir's endpoint.\n**Action:** Developer changes only the endpoint URL in their existing SDK client. Indexes 10,000 product documents with `product_id` as primary key. Executes 5 distinct search queries.\n**Pass criteria:**\n- HTTP 200 on all 5 queries; response JSON validates against the Meilisearch search response schema\n- API compatibility test suite (§8) passes against both Miroir and a single-node Meilisearch with identical results for each query\n- `GET /_miroir/topology` shows all 3 nodes healthy, `fully_covered: true`\n- No `X-Miroir-Degraded` header on any response\n**Fail criteria:**\n- Any field is absent that Meilisearch would return (`estimatedTotalHits`, `facetDistribution`, etc.)\n- Any standard Meilisearch SDK raises an exception that would not arise against a direct CE instance\n- Search result set differs in top-3 ordering from what single-node Meilisearch returns for identical documents and settings\n\n### Scenario 2: Node Failure Tolerance (HA)\n**Setup:** 3-node cluster, RF=2, RG=1, S=32. 50,000 documents indexed and confirmed searchable.\n**Action:** Operator terminates one of the 3 Meilisearch pods while search and write traffic continues.\n**Pass criteria:**\n- 100% of searches during the failure window return HTTP 200 with complete results; `X-Miroir-Degraded` is absent (RF=2 means surviving replicas cover all shards)\n- Writes during the failure window return HTTP 200 (at least one group reached quorum)\n- `GET /_miroir/topology` transitions from `healthy` → `degraded` within 15s of termination, and back to `healthy` within 15s of recovery\n- Anti-entropy closes any replica divergence introduced during the failure within one scheduled pass\n**Fail criteria:**\n- Any HTTP 503 during the single-node failure window with RF=2 (full shard coverage is maintained)\n- `X-Miroir-Degraded` header appears after the failed node recovers and routing resumes\n\n### Scenario 3: Node Addition Without Downtime (Elastic Scale)\n**Setup:** 2-node cluster, S=16, RF=1, RG=1. 100,000 documents indexed.\n**Action:** Operator scales the Meilisearch StatefulSet to 3 replicas and registers the new node via `POST /_miroir/nodes`.\n**Pass criteria:**\n- Rebalance completes within `migration_timeout_s` for 100k documents\n- `miroir_degraded_shards_total = 0` throughout the rebalance\n- Document count via `GET /indexes/products/stats` is unchanged before and after rebalance\n- No search returns zero results for any document that was indexed before the rebalance\n- `miroir-ctl verify` reports 0 discrepancies after rebalance completes\n**Fail criteria:**\n- Any document present before rebalance is unreachable via search or `GET /documents/{id}` after rebalance\n- `miroir_degraded_shards_total > 0` for more than 5 minutes during migration\n- Any node holds > 60% of total documents after rebalance (non-uniform distribution)\n\n### Scenario 4: Settings Change Propagates Consistently\n**Setup:** 3-node cluster, RF=2, S=32. 10,000 documents indexed.\n**Action:** Operator adds a synonym: `[\"laptop\", \"notebook\"]`. Waits for the Miroir task to reach `succeeded`. Immediately searches for \"notebook\".\n**Pass criteria:**\n- `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` on each Meilisearch node directly returns identical `synonyms` objects after the task succeeds\n- Search for \"notebook\" returns ≥ 1 hit within 5s of task completion\n- `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` is absent on all responses after the task reaches `succeeded`\n- `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total` does not increment after the two-phase broadcast commits\n**Fail criteria:**\n- Any Meilisearch node has different synonyms than others after the commit phase (checked via direct `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings` on each node)\n- Search for \"notebook\" returns zero hits while search for \"laptop\" returns > 0 hits on the same index\n\n### Scenario 5: Migration from Single-Node (Operator Journey)\n**Setup:** Existing single-node Meilisearch CE instance with a ~5 GB corpus, ~500k documents, running in production.\n**Action:** Operator follows the §11 \"dump and reload\" migration path: exports a dump, deploys Miroir + 3-node cluster, imports via `POST /_miroir/dumps/import`.\n**Pass criteria:**\n- `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` via Miroir returns `numberOfDocuments ≥ 499,000` after import task succeeds (< 0.2% loss threshold)\n- 20 randomly-sampled queries return identical top-3 results against both the original instance and Miroir (with identical settings)\n- `miroir-ctl verify` reports 0 discrepancies after import\n- No single node holds > 60% of total documents (shard distribution confirmed)\n**Fail criteria:**\n- `numberOfDocuments` after import < 499,000\n- Any of the 20 sampled queries differs in top-3 hits against Miroir vs. the original instance\n- Any node holds > 60% of documents (broadcast mode accidentally used instead of streaming)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phasing\n\nImplementation is broken into 12 phases. The bead tracker (`.beads/`) is the authoritative task list; this section defines **phase gates only** — the testable criteria that determine when each phase is complete and the next may begin.\n\n### Phase 0: Foundation (Walking Skeleton) — COMPLETE\n**Delivers:** Cargo workspace, all three crates scaffold. Binary compiles, boots, and serves `/health → {\"status\":\"available\"}`. Config struct loads from YAML.\n**Completion criteria (all must pass on same commit):**\n- `cargo build --all` succeeds with zero warnings\n- `GET /health` returns `{\"status\":\"available\"}`\n- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean\n\n### Phase 1: Core Routing — COMPLETE\n**Delivers:** Rendezvous hash, shard assignment, covering-set derivation, result merger. Pure functions — no HTTP.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- `cargo test --package miroir-core` — 100% of router, topology, scatter, merger unit tests pass\n- Determinism: same inputs always produce same shard assignment (unit test)\n- Minimal reshuffling: adding a node moves at most ⌈S/(N+1)⌉ shards (unit test)\n\n### Phase 2: HTTP Proxy + API Surface — COMPLETE\n**Delivers:** Full Meilisearch-compatible HTTP proxy — write fan-out, scatter-gather search, task reconciliation, auth dispatch.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- All route handler integration tests pass against a single-node Meilisearch (RF=1, S=16)\n- Scenario 1 (Invisible Federation) passes manually\n\n### Phase 3: Task Store + Persistence — COMPLETE\n**Delivers:** SQLite task store with all 14 tables. Task IDs survive Miroir restarts.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- SQLite: all tables created on startup; task mapping survives process restart (unit test simulating restart)\n- `cargo test --package miroir-core -- task` passes\n\n### Phase 4: Topology Operations — IN PROGRESS\n**Entry criteria:** Phase 3 complete; task store persists durably.\n**Delivers:** Rebalancer, node add/remove, replica group add/remove, drain, health-check state machine.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- Scenario 3 (Node Addition Without Downtime) passes against a 3-node docker-compose setup\n- Node drain completes with `miroir_degraded_shards_total = 0` after drain finishes\n- `miroir-ctl verify` reports 0 discrepancies after each rebalance\n\n### Phase 5: Advanced Capabilities (§13.1–§13.21) — OPEN\n**Entry criteria:** Phase 4 complete; Scenario 3 passes.\n**Delivers:** §13.1 resharding, §13.5 two-phase broadcast, §13.6 session pinning, §13.7 aliases, §13.8 anti-entropy, §13.9 dump import. All individually feature-flagged; remaining §13.x capabilities follow.\n**Completion criteria (minimum for phase exit):**\n- §13.5: Scenario 4 (Settings Change) passes\n- §13.9: Scenario 5 (Migration) passes for a 100k-document corpus\n- Each enabled capability has ≥ 1 integration test scenario passing\n- Toggling any capability to `enabled: false` restores pre-capability behavior (regression-tested)\n\n### Phase 6: Horizontal Scaling (§14) — OPEN\n**Entry criteria:** Phase 5 complete; §13.5 and §13.9 pass their scenarios.\n**Delivers:** Peer discovery, Mode A/B/C background work partitioning, HPA spec, Redis task store.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- 2-pod Miroir + Redis: anti-entropy, TTL sweeper, and canary runner each run on exactly one pod at a time (verified via `miroir_owned_shards_count` metric)\n- HPA scales from 2 → 4 pods under synthetic load; task ownership redistributes without duplicate work\n- `values.schema.json` rejects `replicas > 1` + `taskStore.backend=sqlite`\n\n### Phase 7: Observability — COMPLETE\n**Delivers:** Core metric families, Grafana dashboard, ServiceMonitor, PrometheusRule.\n\n### Phase 8: Deployment + CI — COMPLETE\n**Delivers:** Dockerfile, Helm chart, Argo Workflows CI template, ArgoCD Application manifest.\n\n### Phase 9: Testing — OPEN\n**Entry criteria:** Phase 6 complete; all capabilities implemented.\n**Delivers:** Full test suite — unit (≥90% core coverage), integration, API compatibility, chaos, property-based, CI-gated benchmarks.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- Unit coverage ≥ 90% for `miroir-core` (tarpaulin gate passes in CI)\n- All 5 acceptance scenarios (§ \"Acceptance Scenarios\") pass\n- All 6 chaos scenarios in §8 pass\n- Property-based tests for router, merger, and filter parser pass (§8 property-based tests)\n- CI benchmark step enforces p99 search latency < 2× single-node on the reference corpus (§8 benchmark denominator) — hard CI failure, not advisory\n- **OP#4 exit criterion:** score normalization validated empirically at ≥ 5M and ≥ 50M documents on the reference corpus before Phase 9 may be declared complete (see §15 OP#4)\n\n### Phase 10: Security + Secrets — OPEN\n**Entry criteria:** Phase 9 complete.\n**Delivers:** Full secret rotation flows, CSRF, scoped Meilisearch key lifecycle.\n**Completion criteria:**\n- All 5 secret rotation procedures in §9 are manually exercised and recorded in CHANGELOG\n- CSRF protection verified via automated cross-origin state-mutating request (rejected 403)\n- `miroir-ctl` non-interactive mode (`--yes` / `MIROIR_NON_INTERACTIVE=1`) works for all destructive commands\n\n### Phase 11: Onboarding + Docs + Artifacts — OPEN\n**Entry criteria:** Phase 10 complete.\n**Delivers:** README, CHANGELOG, SDK examples, public Helm repo.\n**Completion criteria:** Scenario 1 runs end-to-end using only the README quick-start guide, with no prior knowledge of this plan document.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Architecture\n\n### Core model\n\nMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n\n- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n\n```\nClient\n  │\n  ▼\nMiroir Orchestrator\n  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n\n  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1","numLines":300,"startLine":1,"totalLines":4103}}}

\"SELECT current_uid, version, history FROM aliases WHERE name = ?1 AND kind = 'single'\",\n347\t                params![name],\n348\t                |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?, };\n354\t\n355\t        // Build new history\n356\t        let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)?;\n357\t        if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n358\t            history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n359\t                uid: old_uid,\n360\t                flipped_at: now_ms(),\n361\t            });\n362\t        }\n363\t        // Enforce retention bound\n364\t        while history.len() > history_retention {\n365\t            history.remove(0);\n366\t        }\n367\t\n368\t        let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n369\t        let new_version = old_version + 1;\n370\t\n371\t        let rows = tx.execute(\n372\t            \"UPDATE aliases SET current_uid = ?1, version = ?2, history = ?3 WHERE name = ?4\",\n373\t            params![new_uid, new_version, new_history_json, name],\n374\t        )?;\n375\t        tx.commit()?;\n376\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n377\t    }\n378\t\n379\t    fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n380\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n381\t        let rows = conn.execute(\"DELETE FROM aliases WHERE name = ?1\", params![name])?;\n382\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n383\t    }\n384\t\n385\t    // --- Table 4: sessions ---\n386\t\n387\t    fn upsert_session(&self, session: &SessionRow) -> Result<()> {\n388\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n389\t   scope: &str,\n572\t        holder: &str,\n573\t        expires_at: i64,\n574\t        now_ms: i64,\n575\t    ) -> Result<bool> {\n576\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n577\t        let existing: Option<LeaderLeaseRow> = conn\n578\t            .query_row(\n579\t                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n580\t                params![scope],\n581\t                |row| {\n582\t                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n583\t                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n584\t                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n585\t                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n586\t                    })\n587\t                },\n588\t            )\n589\t            .optional()?;\n590\t\n591\t        match existing {\n592\t            None => {\n593\t                conn.execute(\n594\t                    \"INSERT INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n595\t                    params![scope, holder, expires_at],\n596\t                )?;\n597\t                Ok(true)\n598\t            }\n599\t            Some(lease) if lease.holder == holder || lease.expires_at <= now_ms => {\n600\t                let rows = conn.execute(\n601\t                    \"UPDATE leader_lease SET holder = ?1, expires_at = ?2 WHERE scope = ?3\",\n602\t                    params![holder, expires_at, scope],\n603\t                )?;\n604\t                Ok(rows > 0)\n605\t            }\n606\t            Some(_) => Ok(false),\n607\t        }\n608\t    }\n609\t\n610\t    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n611\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n612\t        let rows = conn.execute(\n613\t            \"UPDATE leader_lease SET expires_at = ?1 WHERE scope = ?2 AND holder = ?3\",\n614\t            params![expires_at, scope, holder],\n615\t        )?;\n616\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n617\t    }\n618\t\n619\t    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n620\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n621\t        Ok(conn\n622\t  let rows = conn.execute(\n642\t            \"DELETE FROM tasks WHERE rowid IN (\n643\t                SELECT rowid FROM tasks\n644\t                WHERE created_at < ?1 AND status IN ('succeeded', 'failed', 'canceled')\n645\t                LIMIT ?2\n646\t            )\",\n647\t            params![cutoff_ms, batch_size],\n648\t        )?;\n649\t        Ok(rows)\n650\t    }\n651\t\n652\t    fn   Ok(count as u64)\n656\t    }\n657\t\n658\t    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n659\t\n660\t    fn upsert_canary(&self, canary: &NewCanary) -> Result<()> {\n661\t        let conn = Result<()> {\n743\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n744\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n745\t\n746\t        // Insert the new run\n747\t        tx.execute(\n748\t            \"INSERT INTO canary_runs (canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json)\n749\t             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n750\t            params![\n751\t                run.canary_id,\n752\t                run.ran_at,\n753\t                run.status,\n754\t                run.latency_ms,\n755\t                run.failed_assertions_json,\n756\t            ],\n757\t        )?;\n758\t\n759\t        // Prune old runs to stay within the history limit\n760\t        // We want to keep only the most recent N runs (where N = run_history_limit)\n761\t        // Delete any runs that are NOT among the N most recent\n762\t        let limit = run_history_limit as i64;\n763\t        tx.execute(\n764\t            \"DELETE FROM canary_runs\n765\t             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n766\t               AND ran_at NOT IN (\n767\t                   SELECT ran_at\n768\t                   FROM canary_runs\n769\t                   WHERE canary_id = ?1\n770\t                   ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n771\t                   LIMIT ?2\n772\t               )\",\n773\t            params![run.canary_id, limit],\n774\t        )?;\n775\t\n776\t        tx.commit()?;\n777\t        Ok(())\n778\t    }\n779\t\n780\t    fn get_canary_runs(&self, canary_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRunRow>> {\n781\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n782\t        let mut .update_job_progress(\"job-1\", \"in_progress\", r#\"{\"bytes\": 1024}\"#)\n1438\t            .unwrap());\n1439\t\n1440\t        // Renew claim (heartbeat)\n1441\t r#\"{\"bytes\": 4096}\"#)\n1446\t            .unwrap());\n1447\t    }\n1448\t\n1449\t    #[test]\n1450\t    fn job_list_by_state() {\n1451\t        let store = test_store();\n1452\t\n1453\t        for i in 0..4 {\n1454\t            store\n1455\t                .insert_job(&NewJob {\n1456\t                    id: format!(\"job-{i}\"),\n1457\t                    type_: \"reshard_backfill\".to_string(),\n1458\t                    params: \"{}\".to_string(),\n1459\t                    state: \"queued\".to_string(),\n1460\t                    progress: Table 7: leader_lease ---\n1476\t\n1477\t    #[test]\n1478\t    fn leader_lease_acquire_renew_steal() {\n1479\t        let store = test_store();\n1480\t\n1481\t        // First acquisition (now=0, expires=10000)\n1482\t        assert!(store\n1483\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-a\", 10000, 0)\n1484\t            .unwrap());\n1485\t\n1486\t        // Same holder can re-acquire (now=5000, extends to 15000)\n1487\t        assert!(store\n1488\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-a\", 15000, 5000)\n1489\t            .unwrap());\n1490\t\n1491\t        // Different holder, lease not expired — fails (now=6000, lease=15000)\n1492\t        assert!(!store\n1493\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-b\", 20000, 6000)\n1494\t            .unwrap());\n1495\t\n1496\t        // Lease expired — different holder can steal (now=20000, lease=15000)\n1497\t        assert!(store\n1498\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-b\", 30000, 20000)\n1499\t            .unwrap());\n1500\t\n1501\t        // Renew by current holder\n1502\t        assert!(store.renew_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-b\", 35000).unwrap());\n1503\t\n1504\t        // Wrong holder cannot renew\n1505\t        assert!(!store.renew_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-a\", 35000).unwrap());\n1506\t\n1507\t        // Get lease\n1508\t        let lease = store.get_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n1509\t        assert_eq!(lease.holder, \"pod-b\");\n1510\t        assert_eq!(lease.expires_at, 35000);\n1511\t    }\n1512\t\n1513\t    // --- Migration idempotency ---\n1514\t\n1515\t    #[test]\n1516\t    fn migration_is_idempotent() {\n1517\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap();\n1518\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1519\t\n1520\t        // Insert data to prove it survives re-migration\n1521\t        store\n1522\t            .insert_task(&NewTask {\n1523\t                miroir_id: \"survivor\".to_string(),\n1524\t                created_at: 1,\n1525\t                status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n1526\t                node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n1527\t                error: None,\n1528\t                started_at: None,\n1529\t                finished_at: None,\n1530\t                index_uid: None,\n1531\t                task_type: None,\n1532\t                node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n1533\t            })\n1534\t            .unwrap();\n1535\t\n1536\t        // Run migration again — should be a no-op\n1537\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1538\t\n1539\t        // Data still there\n1540\t        assert!(store.get_task(\"survivor\").unwrap().is_some());\n1541\t    }\n1542\t\n1543\t    #[test]\n1544\t    fn schema_version_recorded() {\n1545\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap();\n1546\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1547\t\n1548\t        let conn = store.conn.lock().unwrap();\n1549\t        let version: i64 = conn\n1550\t            .query_row(\n1551\t                \"SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_versions\",\n1552\t                [],\n1553\t                |row| row.get(0),\n1554\t            )\n1555\t            .unwrap();\n1556\t        assert_eq!(version, registry().max_version());\n1557\t    }\n1558\t\n1559\t    // --- Schema version ahead error ---\n1560\t\n1561\t    #[test]\n1562\t    fn schema_version_ahead_fails() {\n1563\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n1564\t        let path = dir.path().join(\"test.db\");\n1565\t\n1566\t        // Create a store with current binary\n1567\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n1568\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1569\t        drop(store);\n1570\t\n1571\t        // Artificially set schema version ahead of binary\n1572\t        let conn = Connection::open(&path).unwrap();\n1573\t        conn.execute(\n1574\t            \"INSERT INTO schema_versions (version, applied_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n1575\t            params![registry().max_version() + 1, now_ms()],\n1576\t        )\n1577\t        .unwrap();\n1578\t        drop(conn);\n1579\t\n1580\t        // Re-opening should fail with SchemaVersionAhead error\n1581\t        let result = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).and_then(|s| s.migrate());\n1582\t        assert!(result.is_err());\n1583\t        match result.unwrap_err() {\n1584\t            crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n1585\t                store_version,\n1586\t                binary_version,\n1587\t            } => {\n1588\t                assert_eq!(store_version, registry().max_version() + 1);\n1589\t                assert_eq!(binary_version, registry().max_version());\n1590\t            }\n1591\t            _ => panic!(\"expected SchemaVersionAhead error\"),\n1592\t        }\n1593\t    }\n1594\t\n1595\t    // --- WAL mode ---\n1596\t\n1597\t    #[test]\n1598\t    fn wal_mode_enabled() {\n1599\t        let store = |row| row.get(0))\n1603\t            .unwrap();\n1604\t        assert_eq!(mode, \"memory\"); // in-memory DB uses memory mode, which is fine\n1605\t    }\n1606\t\n1607\t    #[test]\n1608\t    fn (single-process) ---\n1622\t\n1623\t    #[test]\n1624\t    fn concurrent_writes_no_deadlock() {\n1625\t        use std::sync::Arc;\n1626\t        use std::thread;\n1627\t\n1628\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n1629\t        let path = dir.path().join(\"concurrent.db\");\n1630\t        let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap());\n1631\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1632\t\n1633\t        let mut handles = vec![];\n1634\t        for i in 0..4 {\n1635\t            let s = Arc::clone(&store);\n1636\t            handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n1637\t                let mut nt = HashMap::new();\n1638\t                nt.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), i as u64);\n1639\t                s.insert_task(&NewTask {\n1640\t                    miroir_id: format!(\"concurrent-{i}\"),\n1641\t                    created_at: i as i64,\n1642\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n1643\t                    node_tasks: nt,\n1644\t                    error: None,\n1645\t                    started_at: None,\n1646\t                    finished_at: None,\n1647\t                    index_uid: None,\n1648\t                    task_type: None,\n1649\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n1650\t                })\n1651\t                .unwrap();\n1652\t            }));\n1653\t        }\n1654\t\n1655\t        for h in handles {\n1656\t            h.join().unwrap();\n1657\t        }\n1658\t\n1659\t        // All 4 tasks should be there\n1660\t        let all = store.list_tasks(&TaskFilter::default()).unwrap();\n1661\t        assert_eq!(all.len(), 4);\n1662\t    }\n1663\t\n1664\t    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n1665\t\n1666\t            .unwrap();\n2108\t        }\n2109\t\n2110\t        // Prune tasks older than 3500ms (should delete tasks 0, 1, 2, 3)\n2111\t        let deleted = store.prune_tasks(3500, resilience test ---\n2320\t\n2321\t    #[test]\n2322\t    fn task_survives_store_reopen() {\n2323\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n2324\t        let path = dir.path().join(\"resilience.db\");\n2325\t\n2326\t        // Phase 1: open, migrate, insert a task\n2327\t        {\n2328\t            let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n2329\t            store.migrate().unwrap();\n2330\t            let mut nt = HashMap::new();\n2331\t            nt.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 42u64);\n2332\t            store\n2333\t                .insert_task(&NewTask {\n2334\t                    miroir_id: \"survivor-task\".to_string(),\n2335\t                    created_at: 1000,\n2336\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n2337\t                    node_tasks: nt,\n2338\t                    error: None,\n2339\t                    started_at: None,\n2340\t                    finished_at: None,\n2341\t                    index_uid: None,\n2342\t                    task_type: None,\n2343\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n2344\t                })\n2345\t                .unwrap();\n2346\t            // Drop store — simulates pod shutdown\n2347\t        }\n2348\t\n2349\t        // Phase 2: reopen the same database file\n2350\t        {\n2351\t            let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n2352\t            store.migrate().unwrap();\n2353\t\n2354\t            // Task survives the close/reopen cycle\n2355\t            let task = store.get_task(\"survivor-task\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2356\t            assert_eq!(task.miroir_id, \"survivor-task\");\n2357\t            assert_eq!(task.status, \"enqueued\");\n2358\t            assert_eq!(task.node_tasks.get(\"node-0\"), Some(&42u64));\n2359\t\n2360\t            // Can continue updating the task\n2361\t            assert!(store.update_task_status(\"survivor-task\", \"processing\").unwrap());\n2362\t            assert!(store.set_task_error(\"survivor-task\", \"recovered\").unwrap());\n2363\t\n2364\t            let updated = store.get_task(\"survivor-task\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2365\t            assert_eq!(updated.status, \"processing\");\n2366\t            assert_eq!(updated.error.as_deref(), Some(\"recovered\"));\n2367\t        }\n2368\t\n2369\t        // Phase 3: reopen again and verify the update stuck\n2370\t        {\n2371\t            let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n2372\t            store.migrate().unwrap();\n2373\t\n2374\t            let task = store.get_task(\"survivor-task\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2375\t            assert_eq!(task.status, \"processing\");\n2376\t            assert_eq!(task.error.as_deref(), Some(\"recovered\"));\n2377\t        }\n2378\t    }\n2379\t\n2380\t    #[test]\n2381\t    fn first so we can query it\n        conn.execute_batch(\n            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_versions (\n                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n                applied_at INTEGER NOT         sql.push_str(&conditions.join(\" AND \"));\n        }\n        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY created_at DESC\");\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" new_uid: &str, history_retention: usize) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n\n        // Read current\n        let existing: Option<(String, i64, String)> = tx\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT current_uid, version, history FROM aliases WHERE name = ?1 AND kind = 'single'\",\n                params![name],\n                |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?, row.get(2)?)),\n            )\n            .optional()?;\n        let Some((old_uid, old_version, history_json)) = existing else {\n            return Ok(false);\n        };\n\n        // Build new history\n        let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)?;\n        if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n            history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n                uid: old_uid,\n                flipped_at: now_ms(),\n            });\n        }\n        // Enforce retention bound\n        while history.len() > history_retention {\n            history.remove(0);\n        }\n\n        let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n        let new_version = old_version + 1;\n\n       }\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 7: leader_lease ---\n\n    fn try_acquire_leader_lease(\n        &self,\n        scope: &str,\n        holder: &str,\n        expires_at: i64,\n        now_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let existing: Option<LeaderLeaseRow> = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n                params![scope],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .optional()?;\n\n        match existing {\n            None => {\n                conn.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n                    params![scope, holder, expires_at],\n                )?;\n                Ok(true)\n            }\n            Some(lease) if lease.holder == holder || lease.expires_at <= now_ms => {\n                let rows = conn.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE leader_lease SET holder = ?1, expires_at = ?2 WHERE scope = ?3\",\n                    params![holder, expires_at, scope],\n                )?;\n                Ok(rows > 0)\n            }\n            Some(_) => Ok(false),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = conn.execute(\n            \"UPDATE leader_lease SET expires_at = ?1 WHERE scope = ?2 AND holder = ?3\",\n            params![expires_at, scope, holder],\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Ok(conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n                params![scope],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .optional()?)\n    }\n\n    // --- Tables 8-14: Feature-flagged tables ---\n\n    fn prune_tasks(&self, cutoff_ms: i64, batch_size: u32) -> Result<usize> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        // SQLite doesn't support LIMIT in DELETE directly, so use a subquery\n        let rows = conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM tasks WHERE rowid IN (\n                SELECT rowid FROM tasks\n                WHERE created_at < ?1 AND status IN ('succeeded', 'failed', 'canceled')\n                LIMIT ?2\n            )\",\n            params![cutoff_ms, batch_size],\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows)\n    }\n\n    fn task_count(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tasks\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(count as u64)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n\n    fn upsert_canary(&self, canary: &NewCanary) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO canaries (id, name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7, ?8)\n             ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET\n                name = ?2,\n                index_uid = ?3,\n                interval_s = ?4,\n                query_json = ?5,\n                assertions_json = ?6,\n                enabled = ?7\",\n            params![\n                canary.id,\n                canary.name,\n                canary.index_uid,\n                canary.interval_s,\n                canary.query_json,\n                canary.assertions_json,\n                canary.enabled as i64,\n                canary.created_at,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_canary(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<CanaryRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Ok(conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT id, name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at\n                 FROM canaries WHERE id = ?1\",\n                params![id],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(CanaryRow {\n                        id: row.get(0)?,\n                        name: row.get(1)?,\n                        index_uid: row.get(2)?,\n                        interval_s: row.get(3)?,\n                        query_json: row.get(4)?,\n                        assertions_json: row.get(5)?,\n                        enabled: row.get::<_, i64>(6)? != 0,\n                        created_at: row.get(7)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .optional()?)\n    }\n\n    fn list_canaries(&self) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at\n             FROM canaries\",\n        )?;\n        let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {\n            Ok(CanaryRow {\n                id: row.get(0)?,\n                name: row.get(1)?,\n                index_uid: row.get(2)?,\n                interval_s: row.get(3)?,\n                query_json: row.get(4)?,\n                assertions_json: row.get(5)?,\n                enabled: row.get::<_, i64>(6)? != 0,\n                created_at: row.get(7)?,\n            })\n        })?;\n        let mut result = Vec::new();\n        for row in rows {\n            result.push(row?);\n        }\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    fn delete_canary(&self, id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = conn.execute(\"DELETE FROM canaries WHERE id = ?1\", params![id])?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 9: canary_runs ---\n\n    fn insert_canary_run(&self, run: &NewCanaryRun, run_history_limit: usize) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n\n        // Insert the new run\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO canary_runs (canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n            params![\n                run.canary_id,\n                run.ran_at,\n                run.status,\n                run.latency_ms,\n                run.failed_assertions_json,\n            ],\n        )?;\n\n        // Prune old runs to stay within the history limit\n        // We want to keep only the most recent N runs (where N = run_history_limit)\n        // Delete any runs that are NOT among the N most recent\n        let limit = run_history_limit as i64;\n        tx.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM canary_runs\n             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n               AND ran_at NOT IN (\n                   SELECT ran_at\n                   FROM canary_runs\n                   WHERE canary_id = ?1\n                   ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n                   LIMIT ?2\n               )\",\n            params![run.canary_id, limit],\n        )?;\n\n        tx.commit()?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_canary_runs(&self, canary_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRunRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json\n             FROM canary_runs\n             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n             ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n             LIMIT ?2\",\n        )?;\n        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![canary_id, limit as i64], |row| {\n            Ok(CanaryRunRow {\n                canary_id: row.get(0)?,\n                ran_at: row.get(1)?,\n                status: row.get(2)?,\n                latency_ms: row.get(3)?,\n                failed_assertions_json: row.get(4)?,\n            })\n        })?;\n        let mut result = Vec::new();\n        for row in rows {\n            result.push(row?);\n        }\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 10: cdc_cursors ---\n\n    fn upsert_cdc_cursor(&self, cursor: &NewCdcCursor) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO cdc_cursors (sink_name, index_uid, last_event_seq, updated_at)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)\n             ON CONFLICT(sink_name, index_uid) DO UPDATE SET\n                last_event_seq = ?3,\n                updated_at = ?4\",\n            params![\n                cursor.sink_name,\n                cursor.index_uid,\n                cursor.last_event_seq,\n                cursor.updated_at,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_cdc_cursor(&self, sink_name: &str, index_uid: &str) -> Result<Option<CdcCursorRow>> {\n        let conn =
